AAPL.shape_notes: V3: Deeper 2-dot bite (cols 7-8 empty at rows 4,6). Much more recognizable Apple silhouette. Body curves unchanged — smooth 5→7→7→9 transition.
AAPL.color_notes: Using #A3AAAE (silver-gray). Solid contextual choice for Apple's monochrome identity.

history.notes: Basic apple shape, no bite or leaf
Added bite on right side + offset leaf
Deeper 2-dot bite — key recognizability boost

TSLA.shape_notes: V3: Convex 3-step crown arch (7→9→11 dots) instead of flat top. The arch is the defining Tesla emblem feature. Shoulder notch and stem unchanged.

history.notes: Original T-letter approach
Redesigned as emblem silhouette with crown + stem
Convex crown arch — 3-step taper at top

GOOGL.shape_notes: V2: Consistent 2-dot stroke circle, thinner 4-dot bar (cols 5-8), wider 2-row gap (rows 3-4). Better vertical centering (row 10 empty). Cleaner G legibility overall.
GOOGL.color_notes: Using #4285F4 (Google Blue). Exact primary blue match. Real G uses 4 colors — single-color is a valid simplification.

history.notes: 3-dot thick top, wide bar, narrow gap
Thinner consistent stroke, wider gap, thinner bar

AMZN.shape_notes: V6: Left-high asymmetry — left end starts 1 row higher (row 4) than right end (row 5), matching real Amazon smile. Smoother curve with each point moving exactly 1 col + 1 row. Arrow barb preserved.
AMZN.color_notes: Using #FF9900 (Amazon Orange). Exact match for the smile/arrow color.

history.notes: Letter A with underline — didn't match brand
Diagonal line, not a smile shape
Still too diagonal, not bowing downward
Dropped left-to-right, still not a U-shape
Correct concave bowl smile with arrow tip — user confirmed shape
Left-high asymmetry + smoother curve

MSFT.shape_notes: Four-pane window grid is nearly perfect. Equal quadrants, centered single-dot gap divider. Strongest shape match. Could improve: add 4-color quadrants.
MSFT.color_notes: Using #00A4EF (one of four Windows pane colors). Could use 4 colors for the 4 quadrants.

META.shape_notes: V3: Perfect bilateral symmetry on both axes. Two round oval loops (left cols 0-4, right cols 6-10) with clear woven X-crossing at center. Rows 1/9 mirror (top/bottom arcs), rows 2/8 (upper/lower inner curves), rows 3/7 (2-dot-wide sides + crossing convergence at col 5), rows 4/6 (crossing arms at cols 4,6), row 5 (crossed strands diverged to cols 3,7). The weave pattern creates a natural figure-eight read. Could improve: subtle stroke width variation at loop extremes.
META.color_notes: Using #0081FB (Meta blue). Exact match. Real logo has gradient which can't be represented.

history.notes: Infinity shape was too angular and hard to read
Wider loops, clearer center crossing
Perfect bilateral symmetry, round oval loops, woven X-crossing

NVDA.shape_notes: V4: Thicker eye outline with bolder 2-dot pupil swoosh (rows 3-5). Eye tapers more naturally bottom-to-top. Smoother closure at bottom. Reads clearly as NVIDIA eye mark at all sizes.

history.notes: Diagonal parallelogram, didn't read as eye
Asymmetric eye with pointed left, flat right edge
Added 3-dot diagonal pupil/swirl inside the eye
Bolder outline, thicker pupil swoosh, smoother bottom taper

NFLX.shape_notes: Netflix ribbon-N is very well executed. Two vertical bars with clean diagonal slash. Proportions are good. Could improve: slightly thinner diagonal for more ribbon feel.

SPY.shape_notes: V5: Centered S with even stroke weight, smoother curves top and bottom. Upper loop (rows 0-4) and lower loop (rows 6-10) are mirror images. Proper 2-dot stroke throughout with clean open counters. Near-perfect rotational symmetry.
SPY.color_notes: Using #1B365D (deep navy). Reasonable for State Street/SPDR.

history.notes: Lopsided S-curve
Balanced S with better loop proportions
Thin-stroke S with rotational symmetry, proper proportions
Calligraphic weight variation — thicker terminals, thinner center
Even stroke weight, smoother curves, better top/bottom symmetry

improvement_queue.suggestion: Subtle stroke width variation at loop extremes
Near optimal — eye mark reads clearly
